mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes-AMG GT 43 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AMG GT 43 (M139) engine typically costs £4,000–£15,000 supply only depending on the generation and condition. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,500 depending on workshop location. Premium brand engine replacements can reach £7,000–£15,000+. Main dealer new engines can exceed £12,000 supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes-AMG GT 43 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M139 is a technological masterpiece — it is the most powerful production four-cylinder engine in the world. However, it has known issues: broken valve springs, timing chain and tensioner wear, turbocharger issues, oil consumption, and electrical recalls. A senior Mercedes-AMG official admitted the engine 'failed to resonate with traditional customers'. Some consider it 'the worst Mercedes engine ever made', though this is largely due to emotional response to replacing the V8 rather than objective reliability.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes-AMG GT 43?

acceptedAnswer.text: Broken valve springs (Check Engine Light, rough running, difficulty starting), timing chain and tensioner wear (rattling on startup, 'marbles in a can' sound), wiring harness electrical shorts and fire risk (recall), turbocharger issues (lag, boost leaks, seal wear), and higher-than-normal oil consumption (1L per 1,000 km).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes-AMG GT 43 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold: if your AMG GT 43 is worth £60,000–£80,000 and a recon engine is £8,000–£10,000 (10–17%), it's absolutely worth it. The AMG GT is a high-value performance car that justifies significant repair spend. If the vehicle is a tired example with other issues, consider the overall condition carefully.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes-AMG GT 43 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The AMG GT 43 uses the M139 — a 2.0-litre turbocharged inline-four. Early 4-door X290 models (2019–2023) produce 362–367 bhp, while the 2024+ C192 Coupe produces 416 hp with F1-derived electric turbo technology. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the valve springs, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — valve springs can be replaced individually (£1,000–£3,000). This is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement. If you experience a Check Engine Light with rough running, book an inspection immediately — ignoring broken valve springs can lead to valve drop and catastrophic engine failure.

eraMap.title: The GT 43 THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 367 PS (362 bhp) — early 4-door variant with longitudinally-mounted M139
416 hp, RWD, F1 e-turbo — less emotional but technologically brilliant

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market rates. A replacement engine for the AMG GT typically costs between £4,000 and £12,000 for a reconditioned unit, with labour pushing the total cost significantly higher. Premium brand engine replacements can reach £7,000–£15,000+. The GT 43 starts at around £101,685 new, so engine replacement costs are proportionate to the vehicle's value.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an AMG GT 43 engine typically takes 14–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The longitudinal M139 layout and complex electric turbocharger system add significant labour time. Note that the M139 engine in the GT 43 is longitudinally mounted, whereas the same engine in the A45S is transversely mounted.

problems.title: Broken Valve Springs (M139)
problems.symptoms: Check Engine Light (CEL) on the instrument cluster, rough engine operation, difficulty starting the vehicle, malfunctions with the ECO start/stop system. The car may run rough and feel "a bit rough, particularly for a petrol engine".
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — valve spring failure can occur at relatively low mileage.
problems.repairCost: £1,000–£3,000 (valve spring replacement). Dealer costs may be significantly higher.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, broken valve springs can cause catastrophic engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the Check Engine Light comes on with rough running, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: One or more broken valve springs in the cylinder head is a known issue on the M139 engine. The cause can be installation error or material fatigue. Increasing RPM or boost levels over standard are major contributors to valve spring failure. If you experience any rough running or a CEL, have the engine inspected immediately — ignoring the issue can lead to valve drop and catastrophic engine failure.

problems.title: Timing Chain & Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling or slapping noise from the engine, particularly on cold start, metallic "marbles in a can" sound, noticeable power loss, engine backfires, rough idling or driving. A worn tensioner may also cause the check engine light to come on.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — timing chain stretch can occur due to wear and tear.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; if the chain jumps or snaps, it's an interference engine — catastrophic failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on startup, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The timing chain system on the M139 is subject to wear over time. Failed guides can cause the chains to jump, skip a tooth and potentially snap. The tolerances between the valves and pistons on some engines are very narrow, and this disastrous dance between parts is all kept in harmony by the timing chain and tensioners. If you hear any rattling or slapping on startup, especially a "marbles in a can" sound, book an inspection immediately. A timing chain tensioner replacement costs between £628–£834 in the US — UK costs will be comparable or higher.

problems.title: Wiring Harness & Electrical Recalls
problems.symptoms: Risk of electrical short circuit, potential fire risk when the vehicle is parked. The transmission wiring harness connection might not have been correctly remedied during a prior recall repair, exposing the electrical connector to corrosion.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — recall-related issue affects specific production years.
problems.repairCost: Covered under recall — dealers will replace the two-part wiring harness and connector, free of charge.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — recall remedy is free of charge.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check if your vehicle is affected; an electrical short circuit can increase the risk of a fire.

problems.recommendation: Mercedes-Benz has issued multiple recalls for the AMG GT 43 relating to wiring harness issues. The transmission wiring harness connection might not have been correctly remedied during a prior recall repair, which can expose the electrical connector to corrosion, and result in an electrical short-circuit. An overheated ground cable also increases the risk of fire. Check whether your vehicle is affected and have the recall work completed immediately. The 48-volt system adds additional electrical complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: AMG GT 43 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch early — prevents valve drop and catastrophic engine failure
Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Free of charge — check with your dealer immediately
Replace turbo before it causes engine damage
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This AMG GT 43? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using the correct 0W-40 specification
* Recall work completed (wiring harness, valve springs)
* No rattling or slapping on startup — timing chain should be quiet
* No Check Engine Light or rough running — valve springs should be intact
avoidIf: * Rattling or slapping on startup — timing chain or tensioner wear
* Check Engine Light with rough running — broken valve springs
* No record of recall work completed — wiring harness fire risk
* Poor service history — the M139 is a high-performance engine that demands meticulous maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The AMG GT 43 is a technological masterpiece — the most powerful production four-cylinder engine in the world, with F1-derived electric turbo technology. However, it has been met with customer resistance for replacing the V8 in AMG models. Known issues include valve spring failures, timing chain wear, and electrical recalls. With the 70% Mercedes scrap threshold, a £8,000–£10,000 reconditioned engine on an AMG GT 43 valued at £60,000–£80,000 is 10–17% of the car's value — well worth it for a well-maintained example. However, the M139 engine "failed to resonate with traditional customers", and some AMG enthusiasts consider it "the worst Mercedes engine ever made".

cta.label: → Compare AMG GT 43 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes-AMG GT 43 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or slapping (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Check Engine Light — Any CEL with rough running? (valve spring failure)
* Service History — Regular oil changes with 0W-40 specification
* Recall History — Has the wiring harness recall been completed? (fire risk)
* Valve Spring History — Have valve springs been inspected or replaced?
* Turbo Check — Any whistling, smoke, or power loss? (turbocharger issues)
* Oil Consumption — Check for higher-than-normal oil consumption; some users report 1L per 1,000 km
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

engineCodes.matchNote: This table's rows match Section 1B's Era Map exactly.

items.value: Twin-scroll turbocharger with electric exhaust gas turbocharger (F1-derived)
